steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: The passage ends with a participial phrase—”Having 11,486 feet of prominence,”—followed by a comma. The blank requires an independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the rule for participial phrases
steps.content: An introductory participial phrase must logically modify the subject of the independent clause that follows. Choice B places Mount Hayes—the entity that logically has the prominence—as the subject right after the participial phrase. Choices A, C, and D place geographers, the list, or the ranking as the immediate subject, creating dangling modifiers. That matches choice B — B is correct.
